title: Acme procurement: marketplace vs direct — path & timeline
preview:
From: Lisa Grant <lisa.grant@acme-corp.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement-team@acme-corp.com
Date: 2025-10-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Acme procurement question: marketplace vs direct

Hi Hannah,

Thanks for the discussion yesterday. Procurement is trying to decide whether to buy Redwood via AWS Marketplace (we're AWS-first) or sign a direct contract. Can you help summarize the upside/downside from Redwood's perspective for:

- Procurement timeline (PO / SOW / signature cadence)
- Invoicing and billing (consolidated billing, net terms)
- Contract flexibility (custom SLAs, indemnities, data residency language)
- Co-sell or cloud credits implications (is there a revenue share, and does it affect pricing to us?)

We have a fairly short timeline for the POC (targeting Nov 1 start). If Marketplace would let us move faster, that matters.

Would appreciate a short write-up and any docs you can share. cc'd our procurement inbox.

Thanks,
Lisa Grant
Senior Procurement Manager
Acme Corporation

---


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Lisa Grant <lisa.grant@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-10-02T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acme procurement question: marketplace vs direct

Lisa — thanks for the clear ask. Attached two short docs: a one-pager SLA/ops summary for Marketplace vs Direct, and a spreadsheet that explains how co-sell credits and revenue share affect pricing tiers. High level summary below; happy to hop on a quick call to walk through.

Marketplace (AWS/Azure/GCP) — pros:
- Fast procurement: procurement can often place an order and get product access in 3–10 business days depending on internal approval flows.
- Consolidated billing: charges appear on the customer cloud invoice; good for customers that prefer a single vendor bill.
- Standardized T&Cs: easier for procurement to accept because fewer redlines (some customers treat marketplace purchases as "off-the-shelf").
- Co-sell benefits: for AWS/Azure we participate in partner programs that can surface Redwood to internal account teams and sometimes provide co-sell incentives.

Marketplace — cons / caveats:
- Limited custom contract language: most marketplaces disallow deep redlines to indemnity/data residency/SLAs. If you need bespoke terms, direct contracting wins.
- Pricing and revenue share: cloud marketplaces typically retain a 3–20% take rate (varies by program and cloud), which impacts list price or partner margins; we can work on net pricing for you but the commercial math differs versus direct.
- SLA and support entitlements: enterprise SLAs and bespoke escalation paths are easier to commit to in a direct agreement.

Direct contract — pros:
- Full commercial flexibility: custom SLAs, security addenda, and data residency commitments can be written into the SOW.
- Pricing: no marketplace take; discounts and committed reserved capacity (Dedicated) can be structured directly.
- Enterprise governance: better for customers requiring audit logs, longer retention, custom KMS/HSM arrangements.

Direct contract — cons:
- Longer timeline: legal + procurement + security reviews typically add 6–12 weeks (could be faster with standardized Redwood templates and pre-authorized terms).
- More negotiation overhead and potential for multiple redline rounds.

Recommendation (given your Nov 1 POC start target):
- If Acme's priority is speed-to-PoC and you do not require custom contract clauses or additional indemnities, go Marketplace for the POC (we can issue a marketplace listing with trial credits and a short SOW).
- If you require contractual commitments (data residency, SOC 2 addendum, specific SLA targets) for the POC, begin a parallel direct contracting track and we can run a phased MSA + SOW with a short-term POC addendum.

Next steps: I attached the two docs — Redwood_Marketplace_SLA_Summary.pdf and CoSell_Program_Terms.xlsx. Jordan, can you add a note on reserved capacity pricing and how that works if Acme later converts to Dedicated?

Happy to schedule a 30-min procurement walk-through this week.

Best,
Hannah Schmitt
Head of GTM Ops (Redwood Inference)
hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0198

Attachments: Redwood_Marketplace_SLA_Summary.pdf (1 page), CoSell_Program_Terms.xlsx (spreadsheet)

---


From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Lisa Grant <lisa.grant@acme-corp.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement-team@acme-corp.com
Date: 2025-10-03T14:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Acme procurement question: marketplace vs direct

Quick add from me on pricing and Dedicated conversion:

- Reserved / Dedicated pricing: if Acme anticipates predictable steady traffic, we can model a Dedicated commit that lowers unit costs materially. That pricing is only available via direct contracting (not marketplace).
- Conversion path: we support a staged conversion — POC on Marketplace, then convert with a simple migration SOW to Dedicated. We'll make sure KV cache and model version pinning are preserved.
- Discounts: we can offer blended discounts when you commit term + capacity; Jordan can run a model.

If you want numbers, share an expected token/hour profile and we’ll produce a 3-tier estimate (POC, pilot, production).

-Jordan
Sr. Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference

source.subject: Next steps: Incident response timelines & BCP evidence (Velocity, Inc.)
messages:
From: Sofia Martinez <sofia.m@velocity.inc>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
Cc: security@velocity.inc
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Hi Neha,

Thanks for taking the time today to walk through Redwood's hosting and IR posture. Quick recap from our side and a couple of asks so we can move through procurement: 

- SOC 2: you mentioned a SOC 2 Type II report produced in late 2024. Can you confirm the scope (production API + console) and whether the report can be shared under NDA?
- Incident response: Velocity requires a guaranteed initial notification of any confirmed data breach within 72 hours and a post-incident summary within 14 days. Can you confirm Redwood's standard breach notification timeline and whether 72 hours is feasible?
- BCP/DR evidence: our auditors will want a few artifacts — DR runbook, recent tabletop summary, and evidence of backup restores. Please let us know which of those you can provide and in what format.

Next steps we propose: once we have the SOC 2 stub and IR timelines we can kick off our vendor questionnaire (we use a short security spreadsheet) and estimate a procurement timeline. Are you able to share the SOC2 stub and a one-page IR summary by EOD Wednesday?

Thanks,
Sofia
VP, IT & Security
Velocity, Inc.

---
Attachment: vendor_questionnaire_short.xlsx (referenced, not attached in thread)

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
To: Sofia Martinez <sofia.m@velocity.inc>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Hi Sofia — great to meet you today. Appreciate the clear asks; summarized below with commitments.

1) SOC 2 Type II (scope): production inference API, Console access, and logging pipeline (ingest + audit logs). We can share a redacted PDF of the Type II report under your standard mutual NDA. We will provide a redacted stub (cover + opinion + scope page) by Wed EOD. Full report requires NDA & POI.

2) Incident response / breach notification: Redwood's standard contractual timeline is:
  - Initial triage and internal notification: within 24 hours of detection
  - Customer notification (confirmed incidents affecting customer data): within 72 hours of confirmation
  - Root-cause analysis & remediation plan: high-level summary within 7 business days, detailed RCA within 30 calendar days (exceptions documented).

We can commit to the 72-hour confirmed-notification window in contract language for Velocity. For critical-severity incidents we also offer a 24-hour executive update when requested.

3) BCP/DR evidence: we can provide a one-page DR runbook summary, the most recent tabletop exercise executive summary (Q1 2025), and a checklist of backup/restore evidence. We'll include a short note on RTO/RPO expectations by service class.

Attachments I will share to the Drive folder and also attach a redacted SOC2 stub here for quick review.

Next steps:
- I'll upload the redacted SOC2 stub + IR one-pager + BCP checklist to the shared Drive by Wed EOD and email you direct links.
- Once you confirm the NDA path for the full report, I'll loop our legal ops to exchange documents.
- Happy to schedule a 30-minute follow-up with our security engineering lead (Ethan) if you want a deeper technical review — suggested times Thu 09:00 or Fri 14:30 PT.

Thanks,
Neha
Enterprise Sales (Solutions) — Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0198

---
Attachment: SOC2_Report_Redwood_2024-11_stub.pdf (redacted stub)

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Forwarding for visibility — I've started drafting the one-page IR summary and the BCP checklist. Notes for the pack:

- IR summary (one pager): TL;DR of detection, escalation, customer notification windows, contact tree, and evidence retention. Include our standard contact list (security@redwood.inference, ir-oncall@redwood.inference).
- BCP Checklist: RTO/RPO table per service class, last tabletop date (2025-03-11), backup frequency, last successful restore (2025-05-02, partial KV-store restore during test).
- SOC2 stub: redacted PDF attached — remove customer names before sharing.

Ethan — can you verify the 'last successful restore' line and confirm the page in the runbook that describes the restore playbook? I want to include a pointer to the runbook section (Runbook v3.4, Section 4.2).

I'll upload the three artifacts to the Drive folder and paste links into HubSpot record for Velocity. Planning to send the external email with links on Wed by 5pm PT.

Monica
Security & Trust Operations — Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.inference

---
Attachment: IR_Plan_Summary.pdf (draft)

From: Sofia Martinez <sofia.m@velocity.inc>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
Cc: security@velocity.inc
Date: Thu, 17 Jul 2025 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Thanks Neha and team — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifications before we route the files to procurement/audit: 

- The 72-hour customer notification window is acceptable; we'll flag the 30-day RCA as 'reasonable' but may request a 14-day summary for incidents that affect production inference clusters (our internal requirement). Would Redwood be open to an accelerated RCA summary for P1 incidents (14 days)?
- For the BCP evidence, our auditors want the last two tabletop summaries. You mentioned Q1 2025 — can you also provide Q3 2024's tabletop executive summary?

If you can drop the Drive links and HubSpot record by close of business Friday we'll start the vendor security questionnaire and coordinate legal. Also please include a contact point for incident escalations (phone + email).

Appreciate the responsiveness — talk soon.

Sofia
VP, IT & Security
Velocity, Inc.

---
Referenced attachments uploaded to Drive: SOC2_Report_Redwood_2024-11_stub.pdf, IR_Plan_Summary.pdf, BCP_DR_Evidence_Checklist.xlsx

- SOC 2: you mentioned a SOC 2 Type II report produced in late 2024. Can you confirm the scope (production API + console) and whether the report can be shared under NDA?
- Incident response: Velocity requires a guaranteed initial notification of any confirmed data breach within 72 hours and a post-incident summary within 14 days. Can you confirm Redwood's standard breach notification timeline and whether 72 hours is feasible?
- BCP/DR evidence: our auditors will want a few artifacts — DR runbook, recent tabletop summary, and evidence of backup restores. Please let us know which of those you can provide and in what format.

Next steps we propose: once we have the SOC 2 stub and IR timelines we can kick off our vendor questionnaire (we use a short security spreadsheet) and estimate a procurement timeline. Are you able to share the SOC2 stub and a one-page IR summary by EOD Wednesday?

Thanks,
Sofia
VP, IT & Security
Velocity, Inc.

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
To: Sofia Martinez <sofia.m@velocity.inc>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 14 Jul 2025 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Hi Sofia — great to meet you today. Appreciate the clear asks; summarized below with commitments.

1) SOC 2 Type II (scope): production inference API, Console access, and logging pipeline (ingest + audit logs). We can share a redacted PDF of the Type II report under your standard mutual NDA. We will provide a redacted stub (cover + opinion + scope page) by Wed EOD. Full report requires NDA & POI.

2) Incident response / breach notification: Redwood's standard contractual timeline is:
  - Initial triage and internal notification: within 24 hours of detection
  - Customer notification (confirmed incidents affecting customer data): within 72 hours of confirmation
  - Root-cause analysis & remediation plan: high-level summary within 7 business days, detailed RCA within 30 calendar days (exceptions documented).

We can commit to the 72-hour confirmed-notification window in contract language for Velocity. For critical-severity incidents we also offer a 24-hour executive update when requested.

3) BCP/DR evidence: we can provide a one-page DR runbook summary, the most recent tabletop exercise executive summary (Q1 2025), and a checklist of backup/restore evidence. We'll include a short note on RTO/RPO expectations by service class.

Attachments I will share to the Drive folder and also attach a redacted SOC2 stub here for quick review.

Next steps:
- I'll upload the redacted SOC2 stub + IR one-pager + BCP checklist to the shared Drive by Wed EOD and email you direct links.
- Once you confirm the NDA path for the full report, I'll loop our legal ops to exchange documents.
- Happy to schedule a 30-minute follow-up with our security engineering lead (Ethan) if you want a deeper technical review — suggested times Thu 09:00 or Fri 14:30 PT.

Thanks,
Neha
Enterprise Sales (Solutions) — Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.inference | +1 (415) 555-0198

---
Attachment: SOC2_Report_Redwood_2024-11_stub.pdf (redacted stub)

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.inference>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 15 Jul 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Forwarding for visibility — I've started drafting the one-page IR summary and the BCP checklist. Notes for the pack:

- IR summary (one pager): TL;DR of detection, escalation, customer notification windows, contact tree, and evidence retention. Include our standard contact list (security@redwood.inference, ir-oncall@redwood.inference).
- BCP Checklist: RTO/RPO table per service class, last tabletop date (2025-03-11), backup frequency, last successful restore (2025-05-02, partial KV-store restore during test).
- SOC2 stub: redacted PDF attached — remove customer names before sharing.

Ethan — can you verify the 'last successful restore' line and confirm the page in the runbook that describes the restore playbook? I want to include a pointer to the runbook section (Runbook v3.4, Section 4.2).

I'll upload the three artifacts to the Drive folder and paste links into HubSpot record for Velocity. Planning to send the external email with links on Wed by 5pm PT.

Monica
Security & Trust Operations — Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.inference

---
Attachment: IR_Plan_Summary.pdf (draft)

From: Sofia Martinez <sofia.m@velocity.inc>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.inference>
Cc: security@velocity.inc
Date: Thu, 17 Jul 2025 15:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up from today's security discovery call

Thanks Neha and team — this is helpful. A couple quick clarifications before we route the files to procurement/audit: 

- The 72-hour customer notification window is acceptable; we'll flag the 30-day RCA as 'reasonable' but may request a 14-day summary for incidents that affect production inference clusters (our internal requirement). Would Redwood be open to an accelerated RCA summary for P1 incidents (14 days)?
- For the BCP evidence, our auditors want the last two tabletop summaries. You mentioned Q1 2025 — can you also provide Q3 2024's tabletop executive summary?

If you can drop the Drive links and HubSpot record by close of business Friday we'll start the vendor security questionnaire and coordinate legal. Also please include a contact point for incident escalations (phone + email).

Appreciate the responsiveness — talk soon.

Sofia
VP, IT & Security
Velocity, Inc.

---

source.subject: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)
related_links: https://redwoodinference.com/benchmarks (embargoed link to be shared on call)
Calendar hold (ICS): to follow via separate invite
source.body:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>, Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com>, Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)
Attachments: Redwood_Benchmark_Prebrief_OnePager_v1.pdf

Hi Alexandra, Ben, and Marie —

I’m reaching out to offer a short, embargoed pre-brief on an upcoming Redwood Inference report we’re publishing on production inference economics.

Working title: "Latency per Dollar: Production LLM Inference Benchmarks Across Models + Regions"
Launch date/time: Wed, July 9 (morning PT)

What we’ll cover (30–45 min):
- Why we built a latency/$ metric (and the supporting latency + throughput + $/1M tokens views)
- What we measured: model/region matrices, workload mix (prefill/decode), concurrency, warmups, caching/batching policy, and stability gates
- The most important takeaways we’re seeing for production teams (especially around p95 behavior and regional consistency)

Embargo (important):
If you accept a pre-brief, we ask that any information shared (slides, data, links, and discussion) remains under embargo until Wed, July 9 at 9:00am PT. Replying “confirmed” is sufficient acknowledgement.

Logistics:
- Format: Zoom
- Attendees from Redwood: PMM lead (Soojin Lee) + an engineering lead for methodology Q&A (as needed)
- We can send the final PDF report + methodology summary 24 hours ahead of the call

A few time options next week (all PT):
- Mon 6/23: 10:00–10:45
- Tue 6/24: 9:30–10:15
- Wed 6/25: 1:00–1:45
- Thu 6/26: 11:00–11:45

If another time works better, feel free to propose.

Thanks,
Ethan

—
Ethan Park
Analyst Relations | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwoodinference.com


On Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT, Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com> wrote:
From: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

Hi Ethan —

Interested. A couple of quick questions:
1) Will the report include any competitor baselines, or is it strictly Redwood environment data?
2) Will you share the full methodology/configs, or a high-level summary only?

If you can do Tue 6/24 at 9:30am PT, that works on my end. 45 minutes preferred.

Thanks,
Alexandra


On Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT, Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com> wrote:
From: Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:18 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

Ethan —

I can do Mon 6/23 at 10am PT. Embargo is fine; please send whatever is shareable beforehand.

Ben


On Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:41 AM PT, Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai> wrote:
From: Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:41 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — sounds relevant.

Wed 6/25 at 1pm PT works for me. Can you confirm who will be on from engineering? I’ll likely have methodology questions.

Marie


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>, Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com>, Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)
Attachments: Redwood_Inference_Benchmark_Methodology_Summary_v0.9.pdf

Alexandra / Ben / Marie —

Great, thank you.

Embargo: Please reply with “confirmed” (or similar) to acknowledge the embargo until Wed, July 9 at 9:00am PT.

On your questions (Alexandra):
1) Competitor baselines: we’re being very conservative here. The report is primarily Redwood-run data across a curated set of open models + regions. Where we include external baselines, they’ll be clearly labeled, with strong caveats (configuration differences, time-of-run, etc.), and not presented as a universal “wins” claim.
2) Methodology: we’ll share a detailed methodology section in the PDF and on the landing page, plus a summary doc (attached) for pre-brief context. We’ll also be prepared to discuss configuration choices (prompt mix, concurrency, warmup, caching/batching policy) and variance/stability checks.

Scheduling:
- Ben: confirmed for Mon 6/23 10:00–10:45am PT
- Alexandra: confirmed for Tue 6/24 9:30–10:15am PT
- Marie: confirmed for Wed 6/25 1:00–1:45pm PT

Engineering attendee:
We’ll have Jonas Weber (Dir Performance & Evals) join for the technical Q&A portions.

Zoom details (same for all three):
https://redwoodinference.zoom.us/j/88419366210

I’ll send calendar invites shortly. We’ll also send the final PDF + a link to the interactive benchmark page 24 hours ahead (still under embargo).

Thanks,
Ethan


On Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 11:29 AM PT, Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com> wrote:
From: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 11:29 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

Confirmed on embargo until July 9 9:00am PT.

Tue 6/24 9:30am PT works. Thanks for the context re: competitor baselines.

Alexandra


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jun 18, 2025 at 8:31 AM PT
Subject: Fwd: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

FYI — all three analysts confirmed times + embargo (see below). I’ll send holds today.

Open item: please confirm Jonas’s availability for Mon 10am PT / Tue 9:30am PT / Wed 1pm PT. If one slot is tight, I can move the engineering portion to the last 15 min.

Ethan

To: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>, Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com>, Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai>

text:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>, Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com>, Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)
Attachments: Redwood_Benchmark_Prebrief_OnePager_v1.pdf

Hi Alexandra, Ben, and Marie —

I’m reaching out to offer a short, embargoed pre-brief on an upcoming Redwood Inference report we’re publishing on production inference economics.

Working title: "Latency per Dollar: Production LLM Inference Benchmarks Across Models + Regions"
Launch date/time: Wed, July 9 (morning PT)

What we’ll cover (30–45 min):
- Why we built a latency/$ metric (and the supporting latency + throughput + $/1M tokens views)
- What we measured: model/region matrices, workload mix (prefill/decode), concurrency, warmups, caching/batching policy, and stability gates
- The most important takeaways we’re seeing for production teams (especially around p95 behavior and regional consistency)

Embargo (important):
If you accept a pre-brief, we ask that any information shared (slides, data, links, and discussion) remains under embargo until Wed, July 9 at 9:00am PT. Replying “confirmed” is sufficient acknowledgement.

Logistics:
- Format: Zoom
- Attendees from Redwood: PMM lead (Soojin Lee) + an engineering lead for methodology Q&A (as needed)
- We can send the final PDF report + methodology summary 24 hours ahead of the call

A few time options next week (all PT):
- Mon 6/23: 10:00–10:45
- Tue 6/24: 9:30–10:15
- Wed 6/25: 1:00–1:45
- Thu 6/26: 11:00–11:45

If another time works better, feel free to propose.

Thanks,
Ethan

—
Ethan Park
Analyst Relations | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwoodinference.com

Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 10:03 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

Hi Ethan —

Interested. A couple of quick questions:
1) Will the report include any competitor baselines, or is it strictly Redwood environment data?
2) Will you share the full methodology/configs, or a high-level summary only?

If you can do Tue 6/24 at 9:30am PT, that works on my end. 45 minutes preferred.

Thanks,
Alexandra

I can do Mon 6/23 at 10am PT. Embargo is fine; please send whatever is shareable beforehand.

Ben

Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — sounds relevant.

Wed 6/25 at 1pm PT works for me. Can you confirm who will be on from engineering? I’ll likely have methodology questions.

Marie


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Alexandra Chen <alex.chen@gigaom.com>, Ben Thompson <ben.thompson@stratechery.com>, Marie Dubois <marie.dubois@theinformed.ai>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwoodinference.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Jun 17, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: Embargoed pre-brief: Redwood Inference Latency/$ Benchmark Report (July 9)
Attachments: Redwood_Inference_Benchmark_Methodology_Summary_v0.9.pdf

Alexandra / Ben / Marie —

Great, thank you.

Embargo: Please reply with “confirmed” (or similar) to acknowledge the embargo until Wed, July 9 at 9:00am PT.

On your questions (Alexandra):
1) Competitor baselines: we’re being very conservative here. The report is primarily Redwood-run data across a curated set of open models + regions. Where we include external baselines, they’ll be clearly labeled, with strong caveats (configuration differences, time-of-run, etc.), and not presented as a universal “wins” claim.
2) Methodology: we’ll share a detailed methodology section in the PDF and on the landing page, plus a summary doc (attached) for pre-brief context. We’ll also be prepared to discuss configuration choices (prompt mix, concurrency, warmup, caching/batching policy) and variance/stability checks.

Scheduling:
- Ben: confirmed for Mon 6/23 10:00–10:45am PT
- Alexandra: confirmed for Tue 6/24 9:30–10:15am PT
- Marie: confirmed for Wed 6/25 1:00–1:45pm PT

Engineering attendee:
We’ll have Jonas Weber (Dir Performance & Evals) join for the technical Q&A portions.

Zoom details (same for all three):
https://redwoodinference.zoom.us/j/88419366210

I’ll send calendar invites shortly. We’ll also send the final PDF + a link to the interactive benchmark page 24 hours ahead (still under embargo).

Thanks,
Ethan

source.subject: MedTechCo retention exception negotiation: PII & log policies
messages:
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Jun 2025 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options

Hi Aisha —

Thanks again for the call earlier today. Quick recap + next steps on the PII/retention topics we covered: 

Summary of ask (from your team)
- PII must not be retained beyond request processing (effectively 0-retention for personal data).
- Embeddings and derived artifacts: need clarity on whether aggregation is acceptable and any minimization rules.
- Audit/logging: shortest possible retention for debug telemetry, with ability to produce audit logs for a 30-day window if required.

What we can offer today (high level) — attached a one-page CSV with options (retention-options-summary.csv):
1) Strict PII 0-retention: payload fields flagged as PII are not written to persistent storage; KV/prefix caching disabled for sessions with PII. (No retention of raw inputs.)
2) Minimal telemetry: anonymized request metadata and aggregated usage counters kept for N days (configurable; standard 7/30/90).
3) Embedding handling: option to generate embeddings but only store ephemeral caches (no persistent embedding store) OR persist hashed embeddings for 30 days under a hashed-only policy.

Tradeoffs to note:
- Option 1 disables certain cross-request caching which can increase token cost and latency for repeat prompts.
- Option 3 (no persistent embeddings) reduces retrieval accuracy for embedding-based searches unless you manage a separate customer store.

Asks / next steps:
- Please confirm which option you want us to pursue (A, B, or C from the CSV) and whether you need an explicit contractual exception clause.
- If Legal needs specific language, flag the scope (fields, datasets, models) and we’ll draft a short exception to attach to the contract.

Attaching: retention-options-summary.csv (summary table)

If it helps I can draft exception language once you confirm the desired technical posture. Karthik and Monica are CC'd to weigh in on the implementation and support plan.

Best,
Vanessa Ortiz
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123

--
Redwood Inference — production-first inference for developers
From: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Legal Ops <legalops@medtechco.health>
Date: Tue, 03 Jun 2025 10:45:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options

Hi Vanessa —

Thanks for the clear summary. From privacy's perspective we need the following as hard constraints before we can proceed to procurement: 

1) PII 0-retention: any request payload fields that contain PII (PHI, SSN, DOB, contact info) cannot be stored in any persistent logs or embeddings. They must be removed or redacted immediately post-processing.
2) Embeddings: No persistent embeddings that contain or could be re-identified to an individual. If Redwood needs embeddings for performance, they must be stored hashed with a salt and expire within 30 days, or we prefer ephemeral-only.
3) Audit access: For compliance we will need an auditable trail that the retention policy is enforced (proof of purge for 0-retention requests) and a way to request short-term debug access under strict controls.

Two follow-ups from Legal: please provide template exception language, and a list of what audit logs Redwood retains by default (fields + retention window). They asked for a doc to review — I attached medtech_privacy_qs.docx with their requests.

If you can provide the exception text and the exact technical controls (e.g., PII-scrubbing pipeline, what metadata is still recorded), we can route to Legal and Privacy for signoff. Once we have draft language Legal can confirm whether an exception is viable.

Aisha
Aisha Khan
Head, Data Privacy | MedTechCo
aisha.khan@medtechco.health

On Mon, Jun 2, 2025 at 3:10 PM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message omitted)
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Jun 2025 13:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options

Aisha —

Appreciate the specificity. Attached is a short draft exception we can propose to Legal (redwood_exception_draft.docx) that captures: 0-retention for PII fields, hashed embeddings with 30-day TTL OR ephemeral-only embeddings (customer to choose), and a limited audit window for debug access.

High-level implementation notes (for transparency):
- PII-scrubbing will run at the ingestion layer. If a request triggers the PII rule we can: 1) redact PII before any cache/write, or 2) route that session to a policy that prevents caching entirely.
- Audit purges: we can produce purge receipts (file listing request IDs and timestamps) for 30 days to satisfy traceability requirements.

Karthik / Monica — can you confirm the feasibility and the expected perf/cost impact for Option A (0-retention PII + ephemeral embeddings)? If Legal wants explicit contract text about purge receipts and KMS access, we should include that in the doc.

Once we have your greenlight I’ll send the draft to MedTech Legal for redline.

Thanks,
Vanessa

On Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 10:45 AM Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health> wrote:
> (previous message omitted)
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Jun 2025 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options (technical)

Vanessa / Monica —

Technical feasibility notes and recommended config options for MedTechCo's asks: 

Option A (Recommended for privacy-first):
- PII 0-retention: Implement at ingestion with deterministic scrubbing patterns + customer-provided redaction schema. Do not write raw input to disk; only ephemeral in-memory processing.
- Caching: disable KV/prefix caching for sessions marked as PII; this will increase latency for repeated prompts (median +40ms) and reduce cache-hit token savings (~10-20%).
- Embeddings: support ephemeral-only embeddings (no persistent store) OR hashed embeddings stored with per-customer salt and TTL of 30 days. Hashed storage will require a separate encrypted bucket and rotation schedule.
- Audit receipts: we can emit purge receipts (json lines) and a proof-of-purge timestamp. We can also provide a signed statement from KMS when keys are rotated or deleted.

Impact to cost/SLOs:
- Latency: small increase when caching disabled; falls within Dedicated SLA but may affect micro-latency guarantees for very low-latency endpoints.
- Billing: ephemeral embeddings increase compute cost (more on-demand generation). Hashed embeddings add storage and retrieval cost but are configurable.

If this aligns with Privacy/Legal we can produce an implementation appendix (1-2 pages) describing the retention controls, purge workflow, and audit artifacts. Ethan, any additional compliance bullet points to add?

- Karthik
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 04 Jun 2025 09:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options / exception language

Hi Aisha, Vanessa —

Thanks for the clear back-and-forth. From a Privacy & Compliance perspective we can sign off on the proposed exception with two small edits: 

1) Add explicit language that KMS/HSM-wrapped keys used for any hashed embedding store will be customer-keyed (bring-your-own-key) for MedTechCo production deployments.
2) Define the audit access guardrails: debug access to ephemeral or purged data requires a signed request from MedTechCo Legal and must be executed in a secure environment; access logs are retained for 90 days.

We will prepare a 1-page Implementation Appendix that describes: the PII ingestion scrubbing rules, proof-of-purge artifacts (receipt format), and the embedding/TTL options. Vanessa — once the appendix is attached to the exception redline, please share with MedTech Legal for review.

I attached a quick redline (exception-redline.pdf) and a draft DocuSign envelope — once Legal confirms edits we can route the envelope for signature: https://app.docusign.com/envelope/details/12345

Next steps (tentative):
- Redwood: finalize exception + appendix (Vanessa/Karthik/Engineering) — target EOD Thu 6/5
- MedTech: Legal review and redline back — target Mon 6/9
- Execute DocuSign envelope after signatures

Monica — please prepare the customer success onboarding plan that reflects the non-persistent embeddings option and the support model for debug requests.

Regards,
Ethan Park
Head of Privacy & Compliance | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.ai

Attachment(s): exception-redline.pdf, docusign-envelope-12345

From: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Legal Ops <legalops@medtechco.health>
Date: Tue, 03 Jun 2025 10:45:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options

Hi Vanessa —

Thanks for the clear summary. From privacy's perspective we need the following as hard constraints before we can proceed to procurement: 

1) PII 0-retention: any request payload fields that contain PII (PHI, SSN, DOB, contact info) cannot be stored in any persistent logs or embeddings. They must be removed or redacted immediately post-processing.
2) Embeddings: No persistent embeddings that contain or could be re-identified to an individual. If Redwood needs embeddings for performance, they must be stored hashed with a salt and expire within 30 days, or we prefer ephemeral-only.
3) Audit access: For compliance we will need an auditable trail that the retention policy is enforced (proof of purge for 0-retention requests) and a way to request short-term debug access under strict controls.

Two follow-ups from Legal: please provide template exception language, and a list of what audit logs Redwood retains by default (fields + retention window). They asked for a doc to review — I attached medtech_privacy_qs.docx with their requests.

If you can provide the exception text and the exact technical controls (e.g., PII-scrubbing pipeline, what metadata is still recorded), we can route to Legal and Privacy for signoff. Once we have draft language Legal can confirm whether an exception is viable.

Aisha
Aisha Khan
Head, Data Privacy | MedTechCo
aisha.khan@medtechco.health

On Mon, Jun 2, 2025 at 3:10 PM Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (original message omitted)


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Jun 2025 13:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options

Aisha —

Appreciate the specificity. Attached is a short draft exception we can propose to Legal (redwood_exception_draft.docx) that captures: 0-retention for PII fields, hashed embeddings with 30-day TTL OR ephemeral-only embeddings (customer to choose), and a limited audit window for debug access.

High-level implementation notes (for transparency):
- PII-scrubbing will run at the ingestion layer. If a request triggers the PII rule we can: 1) redact PII before any cache/write, or 2) route that session to a policy that prevents caching entirely.
- Audit purges: we can produce purge receipts (file listing request IDs and timestamps) for 30 days to satisfy traceability requirements.

Karthik / Monica — can you confirm the feasibility and the expected perf/cost impact for Option A (0-retention PII + ephemeral embeddings)? If Legal wants explicit contract text about purge receipts and KMS access, we should include that in the doc.

Once we have your greenlight I’ll send the draft to MedTech Legal for redline.

Thanks,
Vanessa

On Tue, Jun 3, 2025 at 10:45 AM Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health> wrote:
> (previous message omitted)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Jun 2025 14:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options (technical)

Vanessa / Monica —

Technical feasibility notes and recommended config options for MedTechCo's asks: 

Option A (Recommended for privacy-first):
- PII 0-retention: Implement at ingestion with deterministic scrubbing patterns + customer-provided redaction schema. Do not write raw input to disk; only ephemeral in-memory processing.
- Caching: disable KV/prefix caching for sessions marked as PII; this will increase latency for repeated prompts (median +40ms) and reduce cache-hit token savings (~10-20%).
- Embeddings: support ephemeral-only embeddings (no persistent store) OR hashed embeddings stored with per-customer salt and TTL of 30 days. Hashed storage will require a separate encrypted bucket and rotation schedule.
- Audit receipts: we can emit purge receipts (json lines) and a proof-of-purge timestamp. We can also provide a signed statement from KMS when keys are rotated or deleted.

Impact to cost/SLOs:
- Latency: small increase when caching disabled; falls within Dedicated SLA but may affect micro-latency guarantees for very low-latency endpoints.
- Billing: ephemeral embeddings increase compute cost (more on-demand generation). Hashed embeddings add storage and retrieval cost but are configurable.

If this aligns with Privacy/Legal we can produce an implementation appendix (1-2 pages) describing the retention controls, purge workflow, and audit artifacts. Ethan, any additional compliance bullet points to add?

- Karthik
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Khan <aisha.khan@medtechco.health>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 04 Jun 2025 09:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MedTechCo data handling—retention options / exception language

Hi Aisha, Vanessa —

Thanks for the clear back-and-forth. From a Privacy & Compliance perspective we can sign off on the proposed exception with two small edits: 

1) Add explicit language that KMS/HSM-wrapped keys used for any hashed embedding store will be customer-keyed (bring-your-own-key) for MedTechCo production deployments.
2) Define the audit access guardrails: debug access to ephemeral or purged data requires a signed request from MedTechCo Legal and must be executed in a secure environment; access logs are retained for 90 days.

We will prepare a 1-page Implementation Appendix that describes: the PII ingestion scrubbing rules, proof-of-purge artifacts (receipt format), and the embedding/TTL options. Vanessa — once the appendix is attached to the exception redline, please share with MedTech Legal for review.

I attached a quick redline (exception-redline.pdf) and a draft DocuSign envelope — once Legal confirms edits we can route the envelope for signature: https://app.docusign.com/envelope/details/12345

Next steps (tentative):
- Redwood: finalize exception + appendix (Vanessa/Karthik/Engineering) — target EOD Thu 6/5
- MedTech: Legal review and redline back — target Mon 6/9
- Execute DocuSign envelope after signatures

Monica — please prepare the customer success onboarding plan that reflects the non-persistent embeddings option and the support model for debug requests.

Regards,
Ethan Park
Head of Privacy & Compliance | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.ai

Attachment(s): exception-redline.pdf, docusign-envelope-12345

source.subject: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
source.body:
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 9:14 AM PDT
Subject: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
Attachments: AuditExporter_ThreatModel_v0.3.pdf

Team,

Thanks for walking through the audit export threat model yesterday. Capturing notes + required mitigations we need before Security can sign off for limited beta.

Key risks discussed:
1) Secret exposure (Splunk HEC token, Datadog API key, S3 assume-role creds) via logs, metrics labels, config endpoints
2) Data exfil/SSRF via destination configuration (customer-controlled URLs / hosts)
3) Cross-tenant leakage (exporter config sync, checkpoints, DLQ)
4) PII/sensitive data leakage in exported audit events (esp. user agent / IP + any freeform “details” fields)

Required mitigations (must-have for beta enablement):
A. Destination validation + egress controls
- Enforce allowlist patterns per destination type (Splunk HEC hostnames, Datadog intake domain; no arbitrary URLs)
- Reject private IP ranges / link-local / localhost / non-HTTPS
- DNS resolution check should be done at config validation time (control plane) AND guardrail at send time (exporter) to prevent post-config DNS rebinding.
- Add explicit outbound egress allowlist hooks in exporter runtime (we can treat this as “deny by default” except known sinks).

B. Secrets handling
- No raw tokens/keys stored in exporter config. Control plane should store refs only (secret manager/KMS-backed) and exporter fetches via scoped identity.
- Ensure secret material never enters logs (including debug). Scrub headers in error logs.
- Rotation: document how to rotate destination secrets without downtime; exporter should pick up new secret within N minutes.

C. Tenant isolation
- Checkpoint/DLQ storage namespace must be per-tenant AND per-export-config (avoid shared keys).
- Config sync: exporter should always include tenant_id/workspace_id in the authz context when pulling config. No “list all configs then filter in memory”.

D. PII minimization
- Confirm we are not exporting raw prompt text, completion text, headers, etc. Audit export should stick to canonical audit event schema only.
- If including ip_address/user_agent, ensure they’re optional + only on events where policy allows. Please confirm redaction rules are applied before export.

Sign-off criteria (Security):
1) Above A/B/C/D items implemented + reviewed (PR links ok)
2) Unit tests for destination validation edge cases (localhost, 169.254.169.254, RFC1918, punycode, http downgrade)
3) Runbook updated with “pause exports” + “rotate secret” + “destination validation failures” sections
4) Threat model doc updated to v1.0 with “beta scope disclaimers” (hosted/dedicated only; private out of scope)

Can you reply with owners + target dates? If helpful, Kevin/Hanae and I can do a 30 min follow-up review once changes are in.

- Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

Marcus — super helpful, thank you.

Proposed owners/dates:
- A (destination validation + egress hooks): Connor + my team. Target EOW for validation in control plane + exporter-side guard. We’ll implement “destination type”-based validation (Splunk/Datadog fixed domains; S3 is AWS SDK / no URL). For Splunk we’ll support allowlisted FQDNs only (no IP literal).
- B (secrets handling): Hanae, with Benji (not on thread) to wire secret refs end-to-end. Target early next week; we already have secret refs in control plane draft but need to ensure exporter never sees raw values except at send time.
- C (tenant isolation): my team; checkpoint + DLQ prefixes will include tenant_id + export_config_id. We’ll add a guard that rejects missing tenant_id.
- D (PII): Kevin to confirm policy; on platform side we can enforce schema allowlist at export boundary.

Re: DNS rebinding guard at send time — makes sense. We can do a periodic re-resolve + compare with CIDR denylist. Need to be careful about adding latency per batch.

Will post PR links here as they land.

-Ethan


From: Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 11:18 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

Adding a few specifics on secrets so we’re aligned:

- Storage: Control plane stores destination credentials encrypted w/ KMS and returns only a credential_ref to exporter via config sync.
- Retrieval: Exporter uses its service identity to call internal Secrets API “GetCredential(tenant_id, credential_ref)”. We should scope authz to tenant_id + export_config_id.
- Logging: we’ll add a redaction filter for headers/fields named token/api_key/secret/access_key and ensure adapter errors don’t include request headers.
- Rotation: we can support “create new credential + update export config to point to new ref”; exporter will pick up on next config refresh. Suggest default refresh interval 60s for beta.

Ask: for Splunk HEC “test connection” in Console — please ensure the backend does not echo the token back in response payloads (we’ve seen this bug elsewhere).

-Hanae


From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 1:07 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
Attachments: ENG-5189-review-checklist.txt

+1 to all.

PII/policy confirmations:
- IP address + user agent are permitted ONLY for auth/session/security events (login, token creation/revocation, permission changes, export config changes). Not permitted for inference request-level events.
- “details” field must remain structured + allowlisted (no freeform blobs). If any service emits a string payload into details, it should be dropped or replaced with “[REDACTED]”.
- Export boundary should enforce the audit schema v2 allowlist. If an event has unknown keys, exporter should drop those keys and increment a metric.

Also, please ensure we don’t export internal network identifiers (pod IPs, node names) in source.* fields.

-Kevin


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 2:46 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

From SRE side, the “pause exports” / “kill switch” requirement is important and also security-adjacent (limits blast radius if a sink is misconfigured).

Requests:
- Expose an operator control to disable an export config quickly (control plane toggle that exporter honors within 1–2 min).
- Alerts should NOT include destination hostnames/tokens. For destination, include only destination_type + export_config_id.
- Add an explicit metric for validation failures (config rejected) vs delivery failures (adapter).

Once you have the first cut of destination validation, we can do a quick chaos test by forcing downstream 429s + verifying the exporter doesn’t “fail open” to alternate hosts.

-Sean


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 4:37 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

This all looks good.

A couple clarifications for “beta sign-off” so we don’t block on nice-to-haves:

Must-have for enabling the first beta tenants:
- Destination validation (including private-range/localhost/metadata IP protections) + exporter-side enforcement
- Secret refs end-to-end + redaction in logs/errors
- Per-tenant/per-config namespacing for checkpoints + DLQ
- Schema allowlist enforcement at export boundary (drop unknown keys)
- Kill switch / pause exports path documented in runbook

Can land after first beta enablement (but before expanding cohort):
- DNS rebinding continuous monitoring (as long as we have the exporter-side CIDR denylist and disallow IP literals)
- Additional punycode edge-case tests

Please tag me on the PRs for A/B/C so we can do a fast review turnaround.

- Marcus

On Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Marcus — super helpful, thank you.
>
> Proposed owners/dates:
> - A (destination validation + egress hooks): Connor + my team. Target EOW for validation in control plane + exporter-side guard...

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 9:14 AM PDT
Subject: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
Attachments: AuditExporter_ThreatModel_v0.3.pdf

Team,

Thanks for walking through the audit export threat model yesterday. Capturing notes + required mitigations we need before Security can sign off for limited beta.

Key risks discussed:
1) Secret exposure (Splunk HEC token, Datadog API key, S3 assume-role creds) via logs, metrics labels, config endpoints
2) Data exfil/SSRF via destination configuration (customer-controlled URLs / hosts)
3) Cross-tenant leakage (exporter config sync, checkpoints, DLQ)
4) PII/sensitive data leakage in exported audit events (esp. user agent / IP + any freeform “details” fields)

Required mitigations (must-have for beta enablement):
A. Destination validation + egress controls
- Enforce allowlist patterns per destination type (Splunk HEC hostnames, Datadog intake domain; no arbitrary URLs)
- Reject private IP ranges / link-local / localhost / non-HTTPS
- DNS resolution check should be done at config validation time (control plane) AND guardrail at send time (exporter) to prevent post-config DNS rebinding.
- Add explicit outbound egress allowlist hooks in exporter runtime (we can treat this as “deny by default” except known sinks).

B. Secrets handling
- No raw tokens/keys stored in exporter config. Control plane should store refs only (secret manager/KMS-backed) and exporter fetches via scoped identity.
- Ensure secret material never enters logs (including debug). Scrub headers in error logs.
- Rotation: document how to rotate destination secrets without downtime; exporter should pick up new secret within N minutes.

C. Tenant isolation
- Checkpoint/DLQ storage namespace must be per-tenant AND per-export-config (avoid shared keys).
- Config sync: exporter should always include tenant_id/workspace_id in the authz context when pulling config. No “list all configs then filter in memory”.

D. PII minimization
- Confirm we are not exporting raw prompt text, completion text, headers, etc. Audit export should stick to canonical audit event schema only.
- If including ip_address/user_agent, ensure they’re optional + only on events where policy allows. Please confirm redaction rules are applied before export.

Sign-off criteria (Security):
1) Above A/B/C/D items implemented + reviewed (PR links ok)
2) Unit tests for destination validation edge cases (localhost, 169.254.169.254, RFC1918, punycode, http downgrade)
3) Runbook updated with “pause exports” + “rotate secret” + “destination validation failures” sections
4) Threat model doc updated to v1.0 with “beta scope disclaimers” (hosted/dedicated only; private out of scope)

Can you reply with owners + target dates? If helpful, Kevin/Hanae and I can do a 30 min follow-up review once changes are in.

- Marcus

—
Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

Marcus — super helpful, thank you.

Proposed owners/dates:
- A (destination validation + egress hooks): Connor + my team. Target EOW for validation in control plane + exporter-side guard. We’ll implement “destination type”-based validation (Splunk/Datadog fixed domains; S3 is AWS SDK / no URL). For Splunk we’ll support allowlisted FQDNs only (no IP literal).
- B (secrets handling): Hanae, with Benji (not on thread) to wire secret refs end-to-end. Target early next week; we already have secret refs in control plane draft but need to ensure exporter never sees raw values except at send time.
- C (tenant isolation): my team; checkpoint + DLQ prefixes will include tenant_id + export_config_id. We’ll add a guard that rejects missing tenant_id.
- D (PII): Kevin to confirm policy; on platform side we can enforce schema allowlist at export boundary.

Re: DNS rebinding guard at send time — makes sense. We can do a periodic re-resolve + compare with CIDR denylist. Need to be careful about adding latency per batch.

Will post PR links here as they land.

-Ethan


From: Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 11:18 AM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

Adding a few specifics on secrets so we’re aligned:

- Storage: Control plane stores destination credentials encrypted w/ KMS and returns only a credential_ref to exporter via config sync.
- Retrieval: Exporter uses its service identity to call internal Secrets API “GetCredential(tenant_id, credential_ref)”. We should scope authz to tenant_id + export_config_id.
- Logging: we’ll add a redaction filter for headers/fields named token/api_key/secret/access_key and ensure adapter errors don’t include request headers.
- Rotation: we can support “create new credential + update export config to point to new ref”; exporter will pick up on next config refresh. Suggest default refresh interval 60s for beta.

Ask: for Splunk HEC “test connection” in Console — please ensure the backend does not echo the token back in response payloads (we’ve seen this bug elsewhere).

-Hanae


From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 1:07 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)
Attachments: ENG-5189-review-checklist.txt

+1 to all.

PII/policy confirmations:
- IP address + user agent are permitted ONLY for auth/session/security events (login, token creation/revocation, permission changes, export config changes). Not permitted for inference request-level events.
- “details” field must remain structured + allowlisted (no freeform blobs). If any service emits a string payload into details, it should be dropped or replaced with “[REDACTED]”.
- Export boundary should enforce the audit schema v2 allowlist. If an event has unknown keys, exporter should drop those keys and increment a metric.

Also, please ensure we don’t export internal network identifiers (pod IPs, node names) in source.* fields.

-Kevin


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 2:46 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

From SRE side, the “pause exports” / “kill switch” requirement is important and also security-adjacent (limits blast radius if a sink is misconfigured).

Requests:
- Expose an operator control to disable an export config quickly (control plane toggle that exporter honors within 1–2 min).
- Alerts should NOT include destination hostnames/tokens. For destination, include only destination_type + export_config_id.
- Add an explicit metric for validation failures (config rejected) vs delivery failures (adapter).

Once you have the first cut of destination validation, we can do a quick chaos test by forcing downstream 429s + verifying the exporter doesn’t “fail open” to alternate hosts.

-Sean


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>, Hanae Suzuki <hanae.suzuki@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Mar 12, 2025 at 4:37 PM PDT
Subject: Re: Audit exporter threat model review - notes & required mitigations for beta sign-off (ENG-5189)

This all looks good.

A couple clarifications for “beta sign-off” so we don’t block on nice-to-haves:

Must-have for enabling the first beta tenants:
- Destination validation (including private-range/localhost/metadata IP protections) + exporter-side enforcement
- Secret refs end-to-end + redaction in logs/errors
- Per-tenant/per-config namespacing for checkpoints + DLQ
- Schema allowlist enforcement at export boundary (drop unknown keys)
- Kill switch / pause exports path documented in runbook

Can land after first beta enablement (but before expanding cohort):
- DNS rebinding continuous monitoring (as long as we have the exporter-side CIDR denylist and disallow IP literals)
- Additional punycode edge-case tests

Please tag me on the PRs for A/B/C so we can do a fast review turnaround.

- Marcus

On Tue, Mar 11, 2025 at 10:02 AM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Marcus — super helpful, thank you.
>
> Proposed owners/dates:
> - A (destination validation + egress hooks): Connor + my team. Target EOW for validation in control plane + exporter-side guard...

source.subject: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)
source.body:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Hi Marcus/Kevin,

Following up from the security review prep for Hosted canary rollouts GA.

Can you please confirm what we need for GA around audit logs for rollout actions?

Specifically:
1) Event schema: required fields for “who did what, when” + what we include for diffs.
2) Retention: default retention for audit events in Console vs what we commit to contractually.
3) Customer messaging: how we describe this in docs and in security questionnaires (SIEM export, API access, etc.).

Engineering is implementing ENG-9171 / PR-28877 this week and we want to avoid rework.

Questions we’re getting from prospects:
- “Does a rollout approval show who approved and what changed?”
- “Can we prove a rollback happened automatically due to thresholds vs a human?”
- “How long do you retain audit logs and can we export?”

If helpful, I can send a proposed event list for create/start/step-change/pause/approve/rollback/auto-rollback.

Thanks,
Naomi

—
Naomi Feldman
Product Security (GRC)
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Audit_Log_Customer_Messaging_Draft.md (text/markdown)



From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Naomi —

For GA, we need two things to be true:

A) Audit events are unambiguous and attributable
- Actor identity MUST be present and consistent across Console and API actions:
  - actor_type: user | service
  - actor_id (internal principal id)
  - actor_email (when actor_type=user)
  - actor_ip (best-effort)
  - auth_mechanism: session | api_key | oauth (whatever is available in our auth layer)
- Tenant / scope:
  - organization_id
  - project_id (if applicable to Hosted routes)
  - route_id (or route slug)
- Object identity:
  - rollout_id
  - baseline_model_ref + canary_model_ref (or config refs)
  - rollout_state transition (from_state → to_state)
- Timestamp: event_time (RFC3339)

B) Diffs are safe + useful
- We should include a “summary” of changes plus a structured diff where feasible.
- DO NOT log secrets/prompt contents/headers.
- OK to log model version/config identifiers, traffic percentages, schedule, policy settings (max step, min soak, approvals required), stop condition settings.

Minimum event set (must exist):
- rollout.created
- rollout.started
- rollout.step_changed (or rollout.traffic_updated)
- rollout.paused
- rollout.resumed
- rollout.approval_requested
- rollout.approved
- rollout.rollback_initiated
- rollout.rollback_completed
- rollout.auto_stopped (for stop condition triggers)
- rollout.auto_rolled_back (if we do an actual rollback action)

Also: include “reason” for system-triggered actions:
- reason_code: slo_breach | policy_violation | manual | unknown
- reason_details: e.g., “p95 latency +32% vs baseline for 10m” (no raw metric series needed)

Messaging (high level):
- “Audit events capture administrative actions in the Redwood Console and API (create/start/approve/pause/rollback), including actor and timestamp.”
- “Export options: Console download + API (if we have it). SIEM export depends on Enterprise plan.” (Kevin can confirm the current state; we should not over-commit.)

If ENG-9171 is only emitting some subset, we should call that out now.

-Marcus

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 10:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Adding retention/export specifics.

Retention:
- Default in-product audit log retention (Console UI): 30 days for Hosted tenants (standard).
- Extended retention (90/180/365 days) is behind enterprise entitlements. We should phrase as “configurable retention by plan” in customer-facing docs.

Export:
- Today:
  - Console UI: CSV download of filtered audit events (time range + event type).
  - API: we have the generic audit events endpoint (paginated) available to enterprise tenants. (If this isn’t GA-ready, we need to be explicit about what’s available at GA for Hosted canary.)
- SIEM:
  - We support webhook export for enterprise, and we have Splunk guide in progress. Avoid promising “native Splunk integration” in GA notes.
  - For regulated customers asking “immutable logs”: we can state logs are append-only in our audit store; we do not support customer-managed WORM storage today.

Data handling:
- All audit events are metadata only (no prompts, no request payloads). For rollout stop conditions, we can include a “threshold name” + high-level reason details, but not raw traces.

One nuance: actor_ip is not always present for backend actions (e.g., auto-rollback), so for those it should be blank and actor_type=service with service_name.

Kevin

Kevin Osei
Security Engineering (Audit Logging / Compliance)
Redwood Inference



From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 11:28 AM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Thanks — this is very aligned with what we’re implementing.

Current status (ENG-9171 / PR-28877):
- We already emit:
  - rollout.created
  - rollout.started
  - rollout.paused / resumed
  - rollout.step_changed (percent update + step index)
  - rollout.rollback_initiated / completed
- We also emit:
  - rollout.auto_stopped (on stop-condition trigger)
  - rollout.auto_rolled_back (when the orchestrator performs rollback)
- Approval events:
  - We have approval_requested + approved in branch; pending final wiring to the RBAC bundle (rollout-approver role).

Diff payload:
- We’re doing “diff_summary” (human readable) + “diff_fields” (key:value pairs). No nested objects; we flatten keys like:
  - traffic.canary_percent: “5” → “25”
  - guardrail.min_soak_minutes: “30” → “60”
  - stop_condition.p95_latency_regression_pct: “20” → “15”

Actor:
- actor_email is present for Console initiated.
- For service actions, actor_type=service + actor_id=orchestrator + reason_code + reason_details.

Open question for Marcus/Kevin: do we need to include request_id / correlation_id for every event? We have a trace_id in the backend but not sure if we should expose it.

-Dylan

Dylan Brooks
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference



From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 12:11 PM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Dylan —

Correlation id: yes, but it can be “best effort.”

Ask:
- Include an event_id (UUID) and a request_id/trace_id field when available.
- For “service” actor events, include orchestrator_run_id (or similar) so we can correlate back to internal logs during incident response.

We should not expose internal-only trace IDs that could be used to enumerate infra. If you have a customer-safe “request_id” already surfaced elsewhere, reuse that.

Also please confirm the UI shows enough context:
- event_time, actor, action, and summary
- and for approvals: the approver identity

If you have to cut, keep the audit event emission correct; UI polish can follow.

-Marcus



From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 3:48 PM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

This is great — thank you.

I’m going to capture the GA requirements as:

GA audit logging requirements (Hosted canary rollouts):
- Emit audit events for: create/start/step change/pause/resume/approval requested/approved/rollback initiated/rollback completed/auto stop/auto rollback.
- Required fields:
  - event_id
  - event_time
  - event_type
  - organization_id (+ project_id/route_id where applicable)
  - rollout_id
  - actor_type + actor_id + actor_email (if user) + actor_ip (best-effort)
  - from_state/to_state (when applicable)
  - diff_summary + diff_fields (no secrets/prompt content)
  - reason_code + reason_details for system-triggered actions
  - request_id (best-effort) + orchestrator_run_id for service actions

Retention/export (customer messaging):
- “Audit logs are retained for a default period in Console; longer retention and export options are available by plan.”
- Avoid promising native SIEM integrations; we can say “export supported (enterprise) via webhook/API.”

Next steps:
- Dylan/Chloe: please ensure approval events are included before GA flag goes wide.
- Kevin: please sanity check my wording in the doc draft so we don’t accidentally over-commit.
- Marcus: I’ll add the correlation-id guidance and a note about not exposing internal trace IDs.

Attaching the updated event list I’ll reference in the security questionnaire response pack.

Naomi

Attachment: Rollout_Audit_Events_v1.1.pdf (application/pdf)
