From: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 11:28 AM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Thanks — this is very aligned with what we’re implementing.

Current status (ENG-9171 / PR-28877):
- We already emit:
  - rollout.created
  - rollout.started
  - rollout.paused / resumed
  - rollout.step_changed (percent update + step index)
  - rollout.rollback_initiated / completed
- We also emit:
  - rollout.auto_stopped (on stop-condition trigger)
  - rollout.auto_rolled_back (when the orchestrator performs rollback)
- Approval events:
  - We have approval_requested + approved in branch; pending final wiring to the RBAC bundle (rollout-approver role).

Diff payload:
- We’re doing “diff_summary” (human readable) + “diff_fields” (key:value pairs). No nested objects; we flatten keys like:
  - traffic.canary_percent: “5” → “25”
  - guardrail.min_soak_minutes: “30” → “60”
  - stop_condition.p95_latency_regression_pct: “20” → “15”

Actor:
- actor_email is present for Console initiated.
- For service actions, actor_type=service + actor_id=orchestrator + reason_code + reason_details.

Open question for Marcus/Kevin: do we need to include request_id / correlation_id for every event? We have a trace_id in the backend but not sure if we should expose it.

-Dylan

Dylan Brooks
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference



From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 12:11 PM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Dylan —

Correlation id: yes, but it can be “best effort.”

Ask:
- Include an event_id (UUID) and a request_id/trace_id field when available.
- For “service” actor events, include orchestrator_run_id (or similar) so we can correlate back to internal logs during incident response.

We should not expose internal-only trace IDs that could be used to enumerate infra. If you have a customer-safe “request_id” already surfaced elsewhere, reuse that.

Also please confirm the UI shows enough context:
- event_time, actor, action, and summary
- and for approvals: the approver identity

If you have to cut, keep the audit event emission correct; UI polish can follow.

-Marcus



From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 3:48 PM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

This is great — thank you.

I’m going to capture the GA requirements as:

GA audit logging requirements (Hosted canary rollouts):
- Emit audit events for: create/start/step change/pause/resume/approval requested/approved/rollback initiated/rollback completed/auto stop/auto rollback.
- Required fields:
  - event_id
  - event_time
  - event_type
  - organization_id (+ project_id/route_id where applicable)
  - rollout_id
  - actor_type + actor_id + actor_email (if user) + actor_ip (best-effort)
  - from_state/to_state (when applicable)
  - diff_summary + diff_fields (no secrets/prompt content)
  - reason_code + reason_details for system-triggered actions
  - request_id (best-effort) + orchestrator_run_id for service actions

Retention/export (customer messaging):
- “Audit logs are retained for a default period in Console; longer retention and export options are available by plan.”
- Avoid promising native SIEM integrations; we can say “export supported (enterprise) via webhook/API.”

Next steps:
- Dylan/Chloe: please ensure approval events are included before GA flag goes wide.
- Kevin: please sanity check my wording in the doc draft so we don’t accidentally over-commit.
- Marcus: I’ll add the correlation-id guidance and a note about not exposing internal trace IDs.

Attaching the updated event list I’ll reference in the security questionnaire response pack.

Naomi

text:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 9:14 AM PT
Subject: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Hi Marcus/Kevin,

Following up from the security review prep for Hosted canary rollouts GA.

Can you please confirm what we need for GA around audit logs for rollout actions?

Specifically:
1) Event schema: required fields for “who did what, when” + what we include for diffs.
2) Retention: default retention for audit events in Console vs what we commit to contractually.
3) Customer messaging: how we describe this in docs and in security questionnaires (SIEM export, API access, etc.).

Engineering is implementing ENG-9171 / PR-28877 this week and we want to avoid rework.

Questions we’re getting from prospects:
- “Does a rollout approval show who approved and what changed?”
- “Can we prove a rollback happened automatically due to thresholds vs a human?”
- “How long do you retain audit logs and can we export?”

If helpful, I can send a proposed event list for create/start/step-change/pause/approve/rollback/auto-rollback.

Thanks,
Naomi

—
Naomi Feldman
Product Security (GRC)
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Audit_Log_Customer_Messaging_Draft.md (text/markdown)



From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 10:02 AM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Naomi —

For GA, we need two things to be true:

A) Audit events are unambiguous and attributable
- Actor identity MUST be present and consistent across Console and API actions:
  - actor_type: user | service
  - actor_id (internal principal id)
  - actor_email (when actor_type=user)
  - actor_ip (best-effort)
  - auth_mechanism: session | api_key | oauth (whatever is available in our auth layer)
- Tenant / scope:
  - organization_id
  - project_id (if applicable to Hosted routes)
  - route_id (or route slug)
- Object identity:
  - rollout_id
  - baseline_model_ref + canary_model_ref (or config refs)
  - rollout_state transition (from_state → to_state)
- Timestamp: event_time (RFC3339)

B) Diffs are safe + useful
- We should include a “summary” of changes plus a structured diff where feasible.
- DO NOT log secrets/prompt contents/headers.
- OK to log model version/config identifiers, traffic percentages, schedule, policy settings (max step, min soak, approvals required), stop condition settings.

Minimum event set (must exist):
- rollout.created
- rollout.started
- rollout.step_changed (or rollout.traffic_updated)
- rollout.paused
- rollout.resumed
- rollout.approval_requested
- rollout.approved
- rollout.rollback_initiated
- rollout.rollback_completed
- rollout.auto_stopped (for stop condition triggers)
- rollout.auto_rolled_back (if we do an actual rollback action)

Also: include “reason” for system-triggered actions:
- reason_code: slo_breach | policy_violation | manual | unknown
- reason_details: e.g., “p95 latency +32% vs baseline for 10m” (no raw metric series needed)

Messaging (high level):
- “Audit events capture administrative actions in the Redwood Console and API (create/start/approve/pause/rollback), including actor and timestamp.”
- “Export options: Console download + API (if we have it). SIEM export depends on Enterprise plan.” (Kevin can confirm the current state; we should not over-commit.)

If ENG-9171 is only emitting some subset, we should call that out now.

-Marcus

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference



From: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan_brooks@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Mar 10, 2025 at 10:37 AM PT
Subject: Re: Audit log requirements for Hosted canary rollout actions (schema + retention + messaging)

Adding retention/export specifics.

Retention:
- Default in-product audit log retention (Console UI): 30 days for Hosted tenants (standard).
- Extended retention (90/180/365 days) is behind enterprise entitlements. We should phrase as “configurable retention by plan” in customer-facing docs.

Export:
- Today:
  - Console UI: CSV download of filtered audit events (time range + event type).
  - API: we have the generic audit events endpoint (paginated) available to enterprise tenants. (If this isn’t GA-ready, we need to be explicit about what’s available at GA for Hosted canary.)
- SIEM:
  - We support webhook export for enterprise, and we have Splunk guide in progress. Avoid promising “native Splunk integration” in GA notes.
  - For regulated customers asking “immutable logs”: we can state logs are append-only in our audit store; we do not support customer-managed WORM storage today.

Data handling:
- All audit events are metadata only (no prompts, no request payloads). For rollout stop conditions, we can include a “threshold name” + high-level reason details, but not raw traces.

One nuance: actor_ip is not always present for backend actions (e.g., auto-rollback), so for those it should be blank and actor_type=service with service_name.

Kevin

Kevin Osei
Security Engineering (Audit Logging / Compliance)
Redwood Inference

Actor:

- For service actions, actor_type=service + actor_id=orchestrator + reason_code + reason_details.

Dylan Brooks
Engineering Lead, Console & Tooling
Redwood Inference



From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwoodinference.com>

Cc: Kevin Osei <kevin_osei@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Chloe Martin <chloe_martin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>, Jordan Lee <jordan_lee@redwoodinference.com>

source.subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)
source.body:
From: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Hi all — requesting final security/compliance approval on the permissions + audit approach for Incident Bot v2 before we merge the remaining PRs and enable beta for SRE rotation.

Summary of proposed model (details in attached PDF + scope matrix):

1) Service identity + scopes (least privilege)
- Bot runs as service identity: svc-incident-bot
- Two new internal scopes:
  - incident_bot:config_diff:read (customer-safe, redacted output only)
  - incident_bot:telemetry:read_privileged (bounded query templates; tenant-aware guardrails)
- No write scopes. No direct access to raw secret stores.

2) Config diff endpoint access
- Endpoint returns ONLY allowlisted fields by default; everything else is either omitted or redacted.
- Redaction layer runs server-side (not in Slack bot) to prevent leakage if Slack rendering changes.
- For Sev0/Sev1 only: break-glass path allows on-call to request an expanded diff view (still redacted for secrets, but includes more non-secret fields).
  - This requires incident_id + reason, plus elevated on-call role.

3) Privileged telemetry access
- Query service exposes template-based queries (no arbitrary PromQL/SQL).
- Hard limits:
  - time window max 30m (defaults to 15m)
  - max series/rows per query (bounded)
  - per-incident caching + coalescing
  - rate limits + circuit breaker during upstream slowness

4) Audit logging
- Audit event emitted for:
  - every config diff request (including tenant/service identifiers + incident_id)
  - every privileged telemetry request (including template id + bounds)
  - every break-glass elevation attempt (approved/denied)
- Audit events go to SIEM pipeline with retention aligned to current internal standard.

Open items we want sign-off on today:
- Is the break-glass flow acceptable as described (Sev0/Sev1 only, logged, time-limited)?
- Are the audit event fields sufficient for later review/investigation?

Attachments:
- incident-bot-v2-permissions-audit-approval-summary-v3.pdf
- incident-bot-scope-matrix-v2.xlsx

If approved, Ava will update ADR-013 with final language and we’ll proceed with merges for PR-29133 / PR-29140.

Thanks,
Kaitlyn
Authn/Authz & Tenant Isolation
Redwood Inference


---

From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Approved from Audit Logging / SIEM side with 2 conditions:

1) Ensure audit events include immutable identifiers
- actor_type=service
- actor_id=svc-incident-bot (and any delegated user for break-glass)
- incident_id (required)
- tenant_id OR “global”
- resource_type (config_diff | telemetry)
- resource_id (e.g., config namespace + env; telemetry template id)
- decision (allowed/denied)

2) Retention + review hooks
- Route these events into the existing audit stream (same retention policy as other privileged reads).
- Add a weekly automated report for break-glass usage (count + incident_id + approver), at least during beta.

Break-glass flow as written (Sev0/Sev1, time-limited, fully logged) is acceptable.

If you want, I can help review the event schema in PR-29133 before merge.

Kevin
Audit Logging / SIEM Integration
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

+1 to Kevin’s conditions.

Security approval granted for the permissioning approach with the following notes to capture in ADR-013:

- Redaction must be enforced server-side for config diffs (you already called this out) and covered by unit tests (negative tests for known secret patterns and a small allowlist test).
- For telemetry: keep the template list small for beta (avoid “footgun” templates that explode cardinality) and require explicit bounds.
- For break-glass: require an incident severity field (Sev0/Sev1) and make the time-limited role grant explicit (e.g., expires in 60 minutes).

No objections to moving forward.

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:03 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Thanks all — capturing this as final approval to proceed.

Decision:
- Approved to ship Incident Bot v2 with the proposed least-privilege scopes for config diff + privileged telemetry.
- Break-glass is approved for Sev0/Sev1 only, time-limited, and fully audited.

Conditions (will be tracked as merge blockers for beta):
1) Audit event schema includes the identifiers Kevin listed, plus decision=allowed/denied.
2) Add a weekly break-glass usage report during beta.
3) ADR-013 updated to explicitly state:
   - server-side redaction + test coverage requirements
   - telemetry template approach + bounded query constraints
   - break-glass grant expiry (60 minutes) and severity requirement

Next steps:
- Kaitlyn/Ethan: incorporate notes into ADR-013 today.
- Kevin: please take a pass on PR-29133 event fields before we merge.
- Rafael: once merged, we’ll greenlight SRE beta cohort behind the feature flag.

Related work:
- INT-3312 (bot missing scope) should be resolved by the scope rollout + audit events.

Ava
Platform Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

- Add a weekly automated report for break-glass usage (count + incident_id + approver), at least during beta.

Break-glass flow as written (Sev0/Sev1, time-limited, fully logged) is acceptable.

If you want, I can help review the event schema in PR-29133 before merge.

Kevin
Audit Logging / SIEM Integration
Redwood Inference


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:47 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

+1 to Kevin’s conditions.

Security approval granted for the permissioning approach with the following notes to capture in ADR-013:

- Redaction must be enforced server-side for config diffs (you already called this out) and covered by unit tests (negative tests for known secret patterns and a small allowlist test).
- For telemetry: keep the template list small for beta (avoid “footgun” templates that explode cardinality) and require explicit bounds.
- For break-glass: require an incident severity field (Sev0/Sev1) and make the time-limited role grant explicit (e.g., expires in 60 minutes).

No objections to moving forward.

Marcus Lin
Security Engineering Lead
Redwood Inference


---

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 12:03 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Thanks all — capturing this as final approval to proceed.

Decision:
- Approved to ship Incident Bot v2 with the proposed least-privilege scopes for config diff + privileged telemetry.
- Break-glass is approved for Sev0/Sev1 only, time-limited, and fully audited.

Conditions (will be tracked as merge blockers for beta):
1) Audit event schema includes the identifiers Kevin listed, plus decision=allowed/denied.
2) Add a weekly break-glass usage report during beta.
3) ADR-013 updated to explicitly state:
   - server-side redaction + test coverage requirements
   - telemetry template approach + bounded query constraints
   - break-glass grant expiry (60 minutes) and severity requirement

Next steps:
- Kaitlyn/Ethan: incorporate notes into ADR-013 today.
- Kevin: please take a pass on PR-29133 event fields before we merge.
- Rafael: once merged, we’ll greenlight SRE beta cohort behind the feature flag.

Related work:
- INT-3312 (bot missing scope) should be resolved by the scope rollout + audit events.

Ava
Platform Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Hi all — requesting final security/compliance approval on the permissions + audit approach for Incident Bot v2 before we merge the remaining PRs and enable beta for SRE rotation.

Summary of proposed model (details in attached PDF + scope matrix):

1) Service identity + scopes (least privilege)
- Bot runs as service identity: svc-incident-bot
- Two new internal scopes:
  - incident_bot:config_diff:read (customer-safe, redacted output only)
  - incident_bot:telemetry:read_privileged (bounded query templates; tenant-aware guardrails)
- No write scopes. No direct access to raw secret stores.

2) Config diff endpoint access
- Endpoint returns ONLY allowlisted fields by default; everything else is either omitted or redacted.
- Redaction layer runs server-side (not in Slack bot) to prevent leakage if Slack rendering changes.
- For Sev0/Sev1 only: break-glass path allows on-call to request an expanded diff view (still redacted for secrets, but includes more non-secret fields).
  - This requires incident_id + reason, plus elevated on-call role.

3) Privileged telemetry access
- Query service exposes template-based queries (no arbitrary PromQL/SQL).
- Hard limits:
  - time window max 30m (defaults to 15m)
  - max series/rows per query (bounded)
  - per-incident caching + coalescing
  - rate limits + circuit breaker during upstream slowness

4) Audit logging
- Audit event emitted for:
  - every config diff request (including tenant/service identifiers + incident_id)
  - every privileged telemetry request (including template id + bounds)
  - every break-glass elevation attempt (approved/denied)
- Audit events go to SIEM pipeline with retention aligned to current internal standard.

Open items we want sign-off on today:
- Is the break-glass flow acceptable as described (Sev0/Sev1 only, logged, time-limited)?
- Are the audit event fields sufficient for later review/investigation?

Attachments:
- incident-bot-v2-permissions-audit-approval-summary-v3.pdf
- incident-bot-scope-matrix-v2.xlsx

If approved, Ava will update ADR-013 with final language and we’ll proceed with merges for PR-29133 / PR-29140.

Thanks,
Kaitlyn
Authn/Authz & Tenant Isolation
Redwood Inference


---

From: Kevin Osei <kevin.osei@redwoodinference.com>
To: Kaitlyn Nguyen <kaitlyn.nguyen@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval: Incident Bot v2 permissions + audit logging (config diff + privileged telemetry)

Approved from Audit Logging / SIEM side with 2 conditions:

1) Ensure audit events include immutable identifiers
- actor_type=service
- actor_id=svc-incident-bot (and any delegated user for break-glass)
- incident_id (required)
- tenant_id OR “global”
- resource_type (config_diff | telemetry)
- resource_id (e.g., config namespace + env; telemetry template id)
- decision (allowed/denied)

2) Retention + review hooks
- Route these events into the existing audit stream (same retention policy as other privileged reads).
- Add a weekly automated report for break-glass usage (count + incident_id + approver), at least during beta.

Break-glass flow as written (Sev0/Sev1, time-limited, fully logged) is acceptable.

If you want, I can help review the event schema in PR-29133 before merge.

Kevin
Audit Logging / SIEM Integration
Redwood Inference


---

source.subject: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions
source.content:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@bluepeakins.com>, Tom Wilkes <tom.wilkes@bluepeakins.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PT
Subject: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions

Hi Nina, Tom,

Following up on Fridays call  we want to be precise about what no egress means so we can scope the Private install correctly.

Our current assumptions (please confirm/correct):
- No outbound internet connectivity from the Kubernetes cluster (no NAT, no outbound via proxy).
- Internal-only DNS + internal NTP are OK.
- No public OCSP/CRL checks; certificate validation handled internally.
- All container images must be mirrored into your internal registry (we can provide digests).
- Upgrades delivered as an offline bundle during planned windows (no update checks).

Attached is a short checklist we use for disconnected installs.

Thanks,
Ava

--
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineering
Redwood Inference


On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 10:06 AM PT Nina Shah <nina.shah@bluepeakins.com> wrote:
> Ava  yes, that matches. No outbound via proxy either. Internal DNS/NTP only.
> We also need to understand if any Redwood components attempt telemetry by default.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nina Shah <nina.shah@bluepeakins.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 11:22 AM PT
Subject: Re: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions

Nina  good question. In Private deployments telemetry can be disabled and we do not require any external license checks. The main outbound dependencies to avoid are image pulls and any external cert checks, both handled via your internal endpoints / mirrors.

Well document the exact flags/values for this in the install guide.

-Ben

On Mon, Feb 3, 2025 at 1:48 PM PT Tom Wilkes <tom.wilkes@bluepeakins.com> wrote:
> Confirmed. Also please assume outbound is blocked at the firewall, not just DNS.

title: BluePeak: confirm 'no egress' / disconnected environment assumptions

source.subject: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)
related_links: ENG-4833 (API gateway error contract for residency violations)
PM-1490 (Error messaging contract for policy blocks)
source.body:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

Team —

We need final alignment today so we can lock copy for Console + docs and unblock Gateway implementation.

Open decisions:
1) HTTP status for residency enforcement blocks: 403 vs 409 vs 422
2) Error codes: exact strings + which ones we expose publicly (SDK mapping relies on this)
3) Do we include policy identifiers in the response? (policy_id / project_id / org_id)

Proposed (current draft from Brian + Amara):
- Status: 409
- error.type: "invalid_request_error"
- error.code: "RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION"
- Include: request_id, help_url, region_selected (if any), allowed_regions, primary_region
- Not include: policy_id (but include policy_scope: org|project|api_key)

I’m +1 on 409 + no policy_id, but want Security + GRC sign-off since customers are already asking for “what config is wrong?” and Support wants something to correlate.

Can each of you reply with an explicit YES/NO on:
- 409
- exposing policy_id (opaque) in the payload

If we can’t expose policy_id, what’s the recommended alternative for Support correlation? (request_id only? also emit an audit/log event with policy_id?)

Naomi
Product, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


---

From: Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 9:34 AM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

+1 to 409.

Reasoning: 403 reads like “you are not authorized” which is confusing for customers when they are authenticated but configured incorrectly. 422 is okay but we’ve been using 409 for “policy prevents this request” in other contexts.

On codes: I’d like to keep the public surface minimal:
- RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION (generic)

We can add a sub_reason field that is stable but optional:
- "region_not_allowed"
- "no_eligible_region"
- "fallback_blocked"

Re: policy_id — I recommend NO. Even if opaque, it becomes something customers will screenshot and forward to auditors; then we have to answer what it means + guarantee stability. For correlation, request_id + an internal log/audit entry that includes policy_id is enough.

If we absolutely need something customer-visible, a "policy_scope" + "effective_level" (org/project/key) seems safer than an id.

Brian
API Platform


---

From: Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

+1 on 409.

On payload, gateway can consistently return this for streaming and non-streaming:

{
  "error": {
    "message": "Request blocked by data residency policy. Configure an allowed region that includes 'eu-west-1' or update your pinned region.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "code": "RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION",
    "param": null,
    "request_id": "req_...",
    "help_url": "https://docs.redwoodinference.com/errors#residency_policy_violation",
    "details": {
      "allowed_regions": ["eu-west-1"],
      "primary_region": "eu-west-1",
      "attempted_region": "us-east-1",
      "policy_scope": "project"
    }
  }
}

I’m neutral on policy_id, but would prefer not exposing it unless Security says it’s necessary. If we don’t expose it, we should make sure the enforcement decision log always contains policy_id + scope + “why” so Support can answer fast.

Also: please confirm whether we want attempted_region (where routing wanted to go) vs selected_region (where we would have sent it). In block cases, “attempted” is clearer.

Amara
API Gateway


---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

YES 409.

On policy_id: prefer NO in the public response.

Control plane side: policy ids are internal UUIDs today; they’re not meaningful to customers and we don’t want to commit to stability/format if we migrate policies (org->project defaulting, etc.).

Alternative for correlation:
- Always include request_id in response (already planned)
- Ensure we log: request_id, org_id, project_id, key_id (if available), effective_policy_id, effective_policy_scope, allowed_regions, primary_region, evaluation_outcome, block_reason
- Emit audit log event for policy changes separately (create/update/delete). For request-time blocks, that’s more of a telemetry event than an audit event.

If Support needs to help customers self-serve, “policy_scope” + “allowed_regions” + “primary_region” + “attempted_region” will usually be enough.

Ethan
Platform Control Plane


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 12:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

Security view:
- YES to 409.
- NO to exposing policy_id.

Rationale: Even opaque ids can become “identifiers of security configuration” that customers (and attackers) can probe for differences. Low risk, but also low value.

We should include:
- request_id
- policy_scope
- block_reason (limited, enumerated)
- allowed_regions + attempted_region

We should NOT include:
- org_id/project_id/key_id
- internal region group ids / failover groups

Please make sure the help_url doesn’t encode customer identifiers and that docs don’t imply “contact Support with policy_id” (since we won’t provide it).

Marcus
Security Engineering


---

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 1:26 PM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

From a GRC / auditability perspective:
- 409 is fine.
- Strong preference for NOT exposing policy_id.

For evidence: what we need is (a) deterministic blocking behavior and (b) ability to demonstrate who changed the residency config and when. That’s satisfied by audit events for config changes + enforcement decision logs internally.

If we keep block_reason enumerated and stable, it also helps with customer security questionnaires (“what happens when a request would violate policy?”).

Olga
GRC


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 4:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

Approved as follows:
- HTTP status: 409
- Public error code: RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION
- Do not expose policy_id in customer-facing responses

Conditions:
- Ensure request_id is always returned (including streaming paths)
- Ensure internal logging captures effective_policy_id and block_reason for Support triage
- Keep block_reason values limited and documented (do not leak internal topology)

Naomi — you can consider this final sign-off from Security.

Aisha
CISO
Redwood Inference

To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 10:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

YES 409.

On policy_id: prefer NO in the public response.

Control plane side: policy ids are internal UUIDs today; they’re not meaningful to customers and we don’t want to commit to stability/format if we migrate policies (org->project defaulting, etc.).

Alternative for correlation:
- Always include request_id in response (already planned)
- Ensure we log: request_id, org_id, project_id, key_id (if available), effective_policy_id, effective_policy_scope, allowed_regions, primary_region, evaluation_outcome, block_reason
- Emit audit log event for policy changes separately (create/update/delete). For request-time blocks, that’s more of a telemetry event than an audit event.

If Support needs to help customers self-serve, “policy_scope” + “allowed_regions” + “primary_region” + “attempted_region” will usually be enough.

Ethan
Platform Control Plane


---

From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 12:08 PM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

Security view:
- YES to 409.
- NO to exposing policy_id.

Rationale: Even opaque ids can become “identifiers of security configuration” that customers (and attackers) can probe for differences. Low risk, but also low value.

We should include:
- request_id
- policy_scope
- block_reason (limited, enumerated)
- allowed_regions + attempted_region

We should NOT include:
- org_id/project_id/key_id
- internal region group ids / failover groups

Please make sure the help_url doesn’t encode customer identifiers and that docs don’t imply “contact Support with policy_id” (since we won’t provide it).

Marcus
Security Engineering


---

From: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 1:26 PM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

From a GRC / auditability perspective:
- 409 is fine.
- Strong preference for NOT exposing policy_id.

For evidence: what we need is (a) deterministic blocking behavior and (b) ability to demonstrate who changed the residency config and when. That’s satisfied by audit events for config changes + enforcement decision logs internally.

If we keep block_reason enumerated and stable, it also helps with customer security questionnaires (“what happens when a request would violate policy?”).

Olga
GRC


---

From: Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 4:41 PM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

Approved as follows:
- HTTP status: 409
- Public error code: RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION
- Do not expose policy_id in customer-facing responses

Conditions:
- Ensure request_id is always returned (including streaming paths)
- Ensure internal logging captures effective_policy_id and block_reason for Support triage
- Keep block_reason values limited and documented (do not leak internal topology)

Naomi — you can consider this final sign-off from Security.

Aisha
CISO
Redwood Inference
text:
From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

Team —

We need final alignment today so we can lock copy for Console + docs and unblock Gateway implementation.

Open decisions:
1) HTTP status for residency enforcement blocks: 403 vs 409 vs 422
2) Error codes: exact strings + which ones we expose publicly (SDK mapping relies on this)
3) Do we include policy identifiers in the response? (policy_id / project_id / org_id)

Proposed (current draft from Brian + Amara):
- Status: 409
- error.type: "invalid_request_error"
- error.code: "RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION"
- Include: request_id, help_url, region_selected (if any), allowed_regions, primary_region
- Not include: policy_id (but include policy_scope: org|project|api_key)

I’m +1 on 409 + no policy_id, but want Security + GRC sign-off since customers are already asking for “what config is wrong?” and Support wants something to correlate.

Can each of you reply with an explicit YES/NO on:
- 409
- exposing policy_id (opaque) in the payload

If we can’t expose policy_id, what’s the recommended alternative for Support correlation? (request_id only? also emit an audit/log event with policy_id?)

Naomi
Product, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


---

From: Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 9:34 AM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

+1 to 409.

Reasoning: 403 reads like “you are not authorized” which is confusing for customers when they are authenticated but configured incorrectly. 422 is okay but we’ve been using 409 for “policy prevents this request” in other contexts.

On codes: I’d like to keep the public surface minimal:
- RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION (generic)

We can add a sub_reason field that is stable but optional:
- "region_not_allowed"
- "no_eligible_region"
- "fallback_blocked"

Re: policy_id — I recommend NO. Even if opaque, it becomes something customers will screenshot and forward to auditors; then we have to answer what it means + guarantee stability. For correlation, request_id + an internal log/audit entry that includes policy_id is enough.

If we absolutely need something customer-visible, a "policy_scope" + "effective_level" (org/project/key) seems safer than an id.

Brian
API Platform


---

From: Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Olga Petrov <olga.petrov@redwoodinference.com>, Dr. Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Jan 29, 2025 at 10:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Internal approval: Public error contract for residency/policy blocks (status + codes + policy_id?)

+1 on 409.

On payload, gateway can consistently return this for streaming and non-streaming:

{
  "error": {
    "message": "Request blocked by data residency policy. Configure an allowed region that includes 'eu-west-1' or update your pinned region.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error",
    "code": "RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION",
    "param": null,
    "request_id": "req_...",
    "help_url": "https://docs.redwoodinference.com/errors#residency_policy_violation",
    "details": {
      "allowed_regions": ["eu-west-1"],
      "primary_region": "eu-west-1",
      "attempted_region": "us-east-1",
      "policy_scope": "project"
    }
  }
}

I’m neutral on policy_id, but would prefer not exposing it unless Security says it’s necessary. If we don’t expose it, we should make sure the enforcement decision log always contains policy_id + scope + “why” so Support can answer fast.

Also: please confirm whether we want attempted_region (where routing wanted to go) vs selected_region (where we would have sent it). In block cases, “attempted” is clearer.

Amara
API Gateway


---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>, Amara Diallo <amara.diallo@redwoodinference.com>, Brian Murphy <brian.murphy@redwoodinference.com>

- Emit audit log event for policy changes separately (create/update/delete). For request-time blocks, that’s more of a telemetry event than an audit event.

If Support needs to help customers self-serve, “policy_scope” + “allowed_regions” + “primary_region” + “attempted_region” will usually be enough.

Ethan

source.subject: Post-Discovery: Quick availability for 30-min product preview & stakeholder windows
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@orbitalai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 20 Jun 2024 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-Discovery: Quick availability for 30-min product preview & stakeholder windows

Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our side:
- Key pain: orchestration latency + unpredictable unit costs on production workloads.
- Goals you mentioned: predictable inferencing latency SLOs for key endpoints and clearer cost controls for burst traffic.
- Good fit area: Dedicated pools + cache/batching optimizations we discussed.

Next step we recommend: a 30-minute product preview (high-level demo + one infra question) with the two technical stakeholders you mentioned (Anika and your infra lead). To make scheduling easy, can you pick one of the windows below? We tried to give options that cover both your and our timezones. All times listed in ET.

Option A — Tue Jun 25 10:00–10:30 AM ET (14:00–14:30 UTC)
Option B — Wed Jun 26 3:00–3:30 PM ET (19:00–19:30 UTC)
Option C — Thu Jun 27 11:30–12:00 PM ET (15:30–16:00 UTC)

If none of those work, tell us two 30-minute windows that do and we’ll fold around them.

Attached: post-discovery notes and a rough mapping of how the Dedicated offering would slot into your infra (redwood_post-discovery-notes.pdf, redwood-prelim-mapping.png).

Thanks — looking forward to locking this in.

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwoodinference.com | m: +1 415-555-0137
(attachment: redwood_post-discovery-notes.pdf) (attachment: redwood-prelim-mapping.png)

From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@orbitalai.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Anika Rao <anika.rao@orbitalai.com>
Date: Fri, 21 Jun 2024 09:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Post-Discovery: Quick availability for 30-min product preview & stakeholder windows

Ethan —

Appreciate the concise recap and the options. Anika (cc'd) and I reviewed calendars. A couple notes:
- Anika will handle the infra attendance; our infra lead (Carlos) is out until the 28th, so he can't join until then.
- We still want the 30-min preview to include a short SLA/rollback discussion.

Of your options, Option B (Wed Jun 26 3:00 PM ET) works best for Anika and me. If Carlos must attend, we can push to Mon Jul 1, but we'd prefer not to delay.

If you can confirm Option B, we'll mark it and expect a calendar invite. Also please include a one-paragraph list of the specific infra questions you'd like us to prepare for (so we don't spend the time guessing).

Thanks,
Jordan
Head of Product — Orbital AI
jordan.miles@orbitalai.com

On Thu, Jun 20, 2024 at 3:12 PM Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Jordan —
> 
> Thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap from our side: ...

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@orbitalai.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwoodinference.com>, Anika Rao <anika.rao@orbitalai.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Jun 2024 07:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Post-Discovery: Quick availability for 30-min product preview & stakeholder windows

Jordan — great, thanks for the quick reply and for looping Anika in. Option B is tentatively held on our side.

Quick answers to prep for infra:
1) How we manage cold-start and prefix cache across multiple routes (we'll show KV cache behavior and cache keys mapping).
2) Quantization & perf tradeoffs we expect for your largest models (we'll show a short table + latency delta).
3) Failure modes & automated fallbacks we use when a Dedicated pool hits capacity (routing policies and model variant fallback).

Agenda (30m):
- 0–5m: quick context & success criteria confirmation
- 5–18m: demo (runtime, cache, batching showcase)
- 18–25m: infra Q&A (the three items above)
- 25–30m: next steps / mutual action items

We'll send a calendar invite for Wed Jun 26 3:00–3:30 PM ET and include a short prep doc. If Carlos becomes available and you'd like to shift to Jul 1, let us know and we'll update the invite.

Marissa / Priyom — fyi so we can bring an infra SME to the call.

Ethan
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
(attachment: redwood-meeting-brief.pdf)

From: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@orbitalai.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Anika Rao <anika.rao@orbitalai.com>
Date: Sat, 22 Jun 2024 09:04:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Post-Discovery: Quick availability for 30-min product preview & stakeholder windows

Thanks — that agenda looks good and those infra prep points are exactly what we need. Wed Jun 26 3:00 PM ET confirmed on our side.

I'll expect the calendar invite and the short prep doc. If Carlos can make Jul 1 later, we'll mention it in the calendar comments but don't change the primary booking unless you ask.

See you then,
Jordan
(attachment: none)
